source.title: BSEE Accident Investigation Report - Structural Damage to Crane Top Drive Trolley

context.region: Gulf of Mexico, Alaminos Canyon Block 735

event.top_event: Lost Time Injury - Personnel Contact with Moving Top Drive System
actions_taken: All stop and safety stand down with all crews
Added red barricade chain and sign for permit to work on ladder leading down to carousel upper platform
Sent alert to all rigs with multipurpose tower configuration
Injured person evacuated to UTMB Galveston, Texas
event.summary: On 8 December 2023 at approximately 08:30 am, a Floorhand sustained a lost time injury when struck by the Top Drive System (TDS) on the Noble Globetrotter II at Alaminos Canyon 735. The injured person was positioned on the port pipe racker upper access platform approximately 80 feet above the drill floor, attempting to troubleshoot a slack rope alarm. The injured person failed to notify the Driller of their return to the platform. When the Driller lowered the TDS, the lower port TDS roller assembly contacted the injured person, folding their upper torso around the handrail. The injured person sustained a punctured lung, fractured ribs, and fractured scapula. The incident resulted from inadequate communication, lack of hazard identification on the JSA, absence of engineering barriers, and no visible warnings identifying the moving equipment hazard.
recommendations: Implement engineered barrier preventing access to port pipe racker upper access platform during operations or until safe clearance is granted
Identify TDS moving equipment hazard on all relevant JSAs
Post visible warnings and cautions identifying moving equipment hazards on port pipe racker upper access platform
Establish mandatory communication protocol requiring personnel to notify drill floor of position changes
Review and enhance hazard analysis procedures for all drilling operations

threats.name: Personnel positioned in line of fire of moving TDS
Inadequate communication between drill crew members
Insufficient clearance between TDS and platform handrail
Absence of hazard identification on JSA

consequences.name: Serious bodily injury - punctured lung, fractured ribs, fractured scapula
Lost time injury requiring evacuation and hospitalization

controls.name: Job Safety Analysis (JSA) for drill pipe drifting
controls.lod_basis: Administrative control to identify hazards prior to work

supporting_text: The TDS moving equipment hazard was not identified on the JSA (Job Safety Analysis) used for verifying drill pipe internal diameter. The only moving equipment hazards identified on the JSA were for the pipe racker and the pipe storage carousel.

controls.name: Handrails on port pipe racker upper access platform
controls.lod_basis: Physical barrier to prevent falls from elevated platform

supporting_text: The IP was not required to don a safety harness since the port pipe racker upper access platform is protected by handrails.
the lower port TDS roller assembly contacted the IP folding his upper torso around the outside handrail while the IP's legs remained stationary

controls.name: Slack rope detected alarm system
controls.lod_basis: Automated detection system to alert operators of wire rope issues

supporting_text: a 'slack rope detected' alarm occurred on the port pipe racker
The Driller was able to clear the 'slack rope detected' alarm
the 'slack rope detected' alarmed again. The AD was able to clear the 'slack rope detected' alarm

controls.name: Radio communication between Driller and crew
controls.lod_basis: Communication protocol to maintain awareness of personnel positions

supporting_text: Inadequate communication between IP and Driller, Driller was not aware of the IP's position on the port pipe racker upper access platform.
IP failed to notify the drill floor that they were moving from the port carousel access platform (IP's expected location) back to the port pipe racker upper access platform and into the line of fire
The IP overheard the 'slack rope detected' alarm and returned to the port pipe racker upper access platform without notifying the Driller.

controls.name: Warning signs and cautions for moving equipment hazards
controls.lod_basis: Visual communication to alert personnel of moving equipment hazards

supporting_text: BSEE investigators noticed there were no warnings or cautions identifying the moving equipment hazard on or around the port pipe racker upper access platform.

There was no posted or visible warning, caution or moving equipment advisories on the port pipe racker upper access platform identifying the moving equipment hazard. This may have given the IP a false sense of safety while on the port pipe racker upper access platform.

controls.name: Engineered barrier to restrict access to port pipe racker upper access platform
controls.barrier_role: Physical prevention of personnel access during operations
controls.lod_basis: Engineering control to prevent personnel from entering hazardous area during TDS operations

supporting_text: An engineered barrier preventing access to the port pipe racker upper access platform during operations or until safe clearance is granted would have prevented the injury from occurring.
Inadequate hazard analysis and engineering control. The TDS has a 3-inch clearance between TDS and the handrail of the port pipe racker upper access platform.

controls.name: Driller awareness of personnel positions during TDS operations
controls.lod_basis: Operator responsibility to maintain awareness of personnel in work area

supporting_text: Driller was not aware of the IP's position on the port pipe racker upper access platform.

The Driller raised the drill string with the TDS, the pipe slips disengaged from the drill string, and the Driller began to lower the TDS. When the TDS passed the port pipe racker upper access platform, the lower port TDS roller assembly contacted the IP

notes.rules: JSON output only. mentioned fields must be evidence-based. Use null for unknown values.
